claim_gate.local_claim_scope: CHGNet Distill Accuracy improves the sealed local Ni vacancy offset-recovery canary.
claim_gate.next_evidence_step: Promote this paired local win into a locked cloud canary with external reference values.

preferred_sources: NIST Interatomic Potentials Repository Ni EAM records
OpenKIM Ni defect and diffusion test records
published DFT or experimental vacancy formation and migration references selected before the run

baseline_methods: CHGNet or UMA candidate if the backend imports cleanly
scientific_distill.distill_objective: Reduce deviation from reference vacancy formation or migration observables while preserving stable MD and bounded intervention rates.
hyperribbon_questions: Does Distill reduce long-horizon drift without over-correcting stiff lattice modes?
Do intervention traces concentrate in complement-like defect coordinates rather than bulk compression axes?
Does extra time buy meaningful closeness or only marginal improvement?
scientific_distill.initial_protocol: Create a Ni-fcc supercell, remove one atom, apply controlled thermal/position offsets, run paired baseline and Distill Accuracy MD, then estimate hop rate, diffusion trend, energy drift, and local-force stability.

demos.title: FCC Ni vacancy diffusion from perturbed lattice to Arrhenius observable

viewer.initial_scene: Ni vacancy is highlighted as the missing-site field; atom paths show baseline and Distill side by side with hop events and local-neighbor distortion overlays.
visual_requirements: vacancy site marker derived from measured positions
baseline and Distill trajectories use the same camera and time scale
Arrhenius or hop-rate curve only appears after measured data exists
intervention markers align to real Distill event timestamps

demos.why_we_care: This is an EAM home-turf problem: a classical potential should be strong, so any Distill gain must be earned against a tough baseline rather than by exploiting an easy failure.

claim_gate.next_evidence_step: Run the paired Distill Accuracy artifact against this same measured protocol before public accuracy claims.

preferred_sources: published DFT stacking-fault energy curves for hcp Mg
OpenKIM or NIST Mg EAM/MEAM model records for a classical baseline
experimentally grounded elastic constants used only as secondary checks

baseline_methods: MEAM or EAM reference lane where available
CHGNet or UMA candidate if the backend imports cleanly
scientific_distill.distill_objective: Improve the path-shape error to DFT-like stacking-fault or shear response while refusing corrections that violate stiff-axis cell stability.
hyperribbon_questions: Does the complement residual signal appear in shear/defect coordinates, not only energy-volume?
Does projected correction improve a full path rather than a single scalar endpoint?
Can Distill refuse unstable shear cases before they collapse?
scientific_distill.initial_protocol: Build hcp Mg supercells with basal and prismatic shear offsets, relax or short-run MD under paired baseline and Distill Accuracy, then score stacking-fault energy shape, stress response, and failure modes.

demos.title: HCP Mg slip and stacking-fault response under controlled shear

viewer.initial_scene: A measured hcp cell shears through the slip path with plane guides, force vectors, and a synchronized stacking-fault energy curve.
visual_requirements: cell and atom positions read from measured trajectory frames
baseline and Distill paths show identical applied shear offsets
stiff-axis violation is visually flagged only from scored diagnostics
reference curve is hidden until source values are locked

demos.why_we_care: HCP slip and stacking-fault behavior exposes anisotropic mechanics where simple potentials often fit easy elastic observables but miss the defect pathway.

claim_gate.local_claim_scope: CHGNet Distill Accuracy improves the sealed local Ni vacancy offset-recovery canary.
claim_gate.next_evidence_step: Promote this paired local win into a locked cloud canary with external reference values.

preferred_sources: published DFT Li migration barrier references for olivine LiFePO4
materials-project or equivalent structure provenance selected before the run
open MLIP benchmark references for Li-ion transport if available

baseline_methods: UMA candidate if the backend imports cleanly
scientific_distill.distill_objective: Improve migration-path or site-occupancy accuracy while refusing corrections that create nonphysical Li jumps, bad cells, or exploding forces.
hyperribbon_questions: Does a ribbon learned from simpler lattice observables transfer to multi-element ionic pathways?
Are useful corrections concentrated in soft migration coordinates rather than global cell axes?
Does the refusal policy catch chemically implausible jumps?
scientific_distill.initial_protocol: Use a locked LiFePO4 structure, perturb Li site occupancy or migration coordinate, run paired baseline and Distill Accuracy trajectories, then score migration-path energy, site recovery, and nonphysical event rate.

demos.title: LiFePO4 lithium-channel migration with energy and occupancy checks

viewer.initial_scene: The viewer follows measured Li ions through channels, overlays site occupancy states, and shows baseline versus Distill migration-path error once the reference is locked.
visual_requirements: Li channels are inferred from measured structure and species labels
baseline and Distill use the same reference and timestep normalization
barrier and occupancy plots appear only from measured scores
intervention overlays are generated from Distill runtime events

demos.why_we_care: This is deliberately outside EAM comfort. The value proposition is whether Distill can stabilize a complex ionic migration task where DFT-trained MLIPs matter and simple metallic potentials are the wrong tool.

purpose: Define real, no-mock long-horizon MLIP demonstrations that run scientifically through Distill and visually through the viewer.

scientific_distill.responsibility: Run the MLIP, apply versioned hyper-ribbon policy in-run, score physics observables, emit fault lines, and decide whether the ribbon helped or hurt.

must_emit: visual overlays derived from real frames
clear awaiting-measurement states when artifacts are absent
viewer.responsibility: Render only measured trajectories, convergence curves, intervention overlays, and published-reference deltas from artifacts produced by the scientific lane.
